visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa for those who intends to visit their close family in Norway who is EA/EEU citizens.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This visa for those who intends to visit their close family in Norway who is EA/EEU citizens.

## Visa Fees
For the complete list of visa fees, please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/). Note that the visa fees must be
                                                                        paid online using a credit or debit card.Aside from the visa fee, VFS will collect a service fee of EUR 75 for each visa application. Please note that resubmission courier fee of
                                                                        EUR 67.5 will be charged to cover the cost of sending documents to the Royal Norwegian Embassy in Bangkok, Thailand. VFS accepts payment in cash at the Norway counter in VFS during
                                                                        your appointment date.
Applicants exempted from paying the Visa Fee:
- Children under 6 years
- School pupils, students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training
- Researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research, as per specific rules
- Applicants related to (spouse, descendant or dependent ascendant of) EEA citizen who are exercising their right to free movement (on production of relevant certificates (birth certificate, marriage certificate, etc.)
- Applications for Entry visas (d-visa) for skilled workers (including their family members) who have received a concrete offer of employment.
- In certain cases, the fee may be waived or reduced in accordance with national legislation, when this would serve to promote cultural, foreign policy or development policy interests, other areas of vital public interest, or for humanitarian reasons.
At VFS, you may avail value-added services which are optional. For details please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/mmr/en/nor/buy-services).

## Documents Required
Checklist for family member/friend of EU/EEA citizen
Important Noted: All documents must be in A4 paper size and NO staples, pins.

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Standards:
All visa applications must include one recent photograph in color meeting the compulsory internationally agreed standards.
The basic rules are simple:
- The photo must not be older than 6 months
- Taken against a plain light background
- Size 35mm by 45mm (IMPORTANT: Passport photos in U.S. format are not accepted)
- Face must cover 2/3 of photo and looking straight forward
- Neutral expression please and mouth must be closed (no obvious grinning or raised eyebrows)
- Eyes must be on the middle line or in upper half of the photograph
- Hair must not cover face
- No reflection or glare on your glasses and the frames should not cover your eyes (you may find it easier to remove your glasses)
- The photograph must be in sharp focus and clear
- Have a strong definition between the face and background
- Printed professionally
- Eyes open and clearly visible
- Full head, without any head covering unless it is worn for religious beliefs or medical reasons
- CHILDREN: photographs of children must show a clear image that is a true likeness of the child. The eyes should be open and if you need to support a baby’s head, it is important, that your hand cannot be seen in the photograph.

## Processing Time
The handling time of the various applications will depend on the case load. The handling time is calculated from the moment an application has been registered in the visa handling system.
For Schengen visa, visa applications shall be decided on within 15 calendar days after the embassy or consulate has received the application. The decision period may be extended to 45
                                                                        days when further consideration is needed.

## Download Forms
You must fill out your visa application form by clicking here (https://www.udi.no/en/).

visa_types.overview:
Overview
You may apply for a residence permit for family immigration if you are married to, or are the registered partner or cohabitant or child of someone who lives in Norway. Other types of
                                                                        family members who may apply are parents who have children in Norway, those who are going to marry someone in Norway (fiancées/fiancés), foster children and full siblings.
In order to apply for a residence permit to Norway from Myanmar, you must either be a Myanmar citizen or have had a residence permit Myanmar for at least six months.
visa_types.full_text:

You may apply for a residence permit for family immigration if you are married to, or are the registered partner or cohabitant or child of someone who lives in Norway. Other types of

In order to apply for a residence permit to Norway from Myanmar, you must either be a Myanmar citizen or have had a residence permit Myanmar for at least six months.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
It is possible to get a visa in connection with a business visit in Norway
You can be issued a visa in connection with a business visit if there is an actual commercial relationship between your own company/organisation and the company/organisation in Norway which you wish to visit.
The relationship with the company/organisation in Norway must have been established prior to your entering Norway, and the purpose of your visit must be related to your line of business in your country of origin.
You will need to submit a copy of a medical travel insurance policy that will cover any costs of repatriation on medical grounds, urgent medical care, or emergency hospital treatment.
The insurance policy must be valid for the entire Schengen Area, for the entire duration of your stay, and it must provide minimum cover of €30,000.
Important of Group Applications:
Group applications must indicate the same period of travel and itinerary. Visa applications for all applicants listed in group registration, must be submitted at VFS on the same date.
If one or more applicant are missing to show up in person at VFS, the following documents are required to submit instead:
A letter from the applicant stating that they wish to cancel their online visa application (which was registered as part of the group).
A request letter for a visa fee refunded.
The applicant who is missing to submit their application together with the group should provide the above two letters, together with the group. A new online application can be registered and be submitted as an individual visa application later.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to study in Norway for longer than three months you must apply for a residence permit for students. This section contains information about which studies constitute grounds
                                                                        for a residence permit. There is also information on how to apply and which rights a study permit entails.
For more information about student permits, please click here (http://www.udi.no/Norwegian-Directorate-of-Immigration/Central-topics/Studies/)

## Visa Fees
For the complete list of visa fees for residence permit, please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/). Note that the visa fees must be
                                                                        paid online using a credit or debit card when you fill in the form.
Aside from the visa fee, VFS will collect a service fee of EUR 75 for each visa application. The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay EUR 67.5 to cover the Resubmission Courier. VFS accepts payment in cash at the Norway counter in VFS during your appointment date.
Applicants exempted from paying the Visa Fee:
- Children under 6 years
- School pupils, students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training
- Researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research, as per specific rules
- Applicants related to (spouse, descendant or dependent ascendant of) EEA citizen who are exercising their right to free movement (on production of relevant certificates (birth certificate,
                                                                            marriage certificate, etc.)
- Applications for Entry visas (d-visa) for skilled workers (including their family members) who have received a concrete offer of employment.
- In certain cases, the fee may be waived or reduced in accordance with national legislation, when this would serve to promote cultural, foreign policy or development policy interests,
                                                                            other areas of vital public interest, or for humanitarian reasons.
At VFS, you may avail value-added services which are optional. For details please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/mmr/en/nor/buy-services).

## Documents Required
- University College or University (http://www.udi.no/en/checklists-container/studies-au-pair/checklist-student/?c=mmr)
- Checklist for Upper Secondary (http://www.udi.no/en/checklists-container/studies-au-pair/checklist-student/?c=mmr)
- Students with admission to a folk high school or a religion/belief-based school (bible school) (http://www.udi.no/en/checklists-container/studies-au-pair/checklist-folk-high-school-or-a-denominational-college/?c=mmr)
- Skilled workers who want to take additional education in order for their education to be recognised in Norway (http://www.udi.no/en/checklists-container/studies-au-pair/checklist-additional-education-/?c=mmr)

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Standards:
All visa applications must include one recent photograph in color meeting the compulsory internationally agreed standards.
The basic rules are simple:
- The photo must not be older than 6 months
- Taken against a plain light background
- Size 35mm by 45mm (IMPORTANT: Passport photos in U.S. format are not accepted)
- Face must cover 2/3 of photo and looking straight forward
- Neutral expression please and mouth must be closed (no obvious grinning or raised eyebrows)
- Eyes must be on the middle line or in upper half of the photograph
- Hair must not cover face
- No reflection or glare on your glasses and the frames should not cover your eyes (you may find it easier to remove your glasses)
- The photograph must be in sharp focus and clear
- Have a strong definition between the face and background
- Printed professionally
- Eyes open and clearly visible
- Full head, without any head covering unless it is worn for religious beliefs or medical reasons
- CHILDREN: photographs of children must show a clear image that is a true likeness of the child. The eyes should be open and if you need to support a baby’s head, it is important, that your hand cannot be seen in the photograph.

## Processing Time
For residence permit application, the Embassy will forward your application to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration for considering. Information about case-processing times is intended
                                                                        as a guide. Some cases make take shorter or longer time to process. For details, please check here (http://www.udi.no/Norwegian-Directorate-of-Immigration/Oversiktsider/Case-processing-times/).

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa is solely for the purpose of leisure travel including vacationing, cruising, sightseeing and other recreational activities.
You will need to submit a copy of a medical travel insurance policy that will cover any costs of repatriation on medical grounds, urgent medical care, or emergency hospital treatment.
The insurance policy must be valid for the entire Schengen Area, for the entire duration of your stay, and it must provide minimum cover of €30,000.
Important Information of Group Applications:
Group applications must indicate the same period of travel and itinerary. Visa applications for all applicants listed in group registration, must be submitted at VFS on the same date.
If one or more applicant are missing to show up in person at VFS, the following documents are required to submit instead:
A letter from the applicant stating that they wish to cancel their online visa application (which was registered as part of the group).
A request letter for a visa fee refunded.
The applicant who is missing to submit their application together with the group should provide the above two letters, together with the group. A new online application can be registered and be submitted as an individual visa application later.

A letter from the applicant stating that they wish to cancel their online visa application (which was registered as part of the group).

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The following information applies to those who are seafarer and need the short-stay visa to boarding from Norway port.
You may submit your application up to 09 months before your planned departure.
You will need to submit a copy of a medical travel insurance policy that will cover any costs of repatriation on medical grounds, urgent medical care, or emergency hospital treatment.
The insurance policy must be valid for the entire Schengen Area, for the entire duration of your stay, and it must provide minimum cover of €30,000.
Important Information of Group Applications:
Group applications must indicate the same period of travel and itinerary. Visa applications for all applicants listed in group registration, must be submitted at VFS on the same date.
If one or more applicant are missing to show up in person at VFS, the following documents are required to submit instead:
A letter from the applicant stating that they wish to cancel their online visa application (which was registered as part of the group).
A request letter for a visa fee refunded.
The applicant who is missing to submit their application together with the group should provide the above two letters, together with the group. A new online application can be registered and be submitted as an individual visa application later.

## Visa Fees
For the complete list of visa fees, please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/). Note that the visa fees must be
                                                                        paid online using a credit or debit card.
Aside from the visa fee, VFS will collect a service fee of EUR 75 for each visa application. The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay EUR 67.5 to cover the Resubmission Courier. VFS accepts payment in cash at the Norway counter in VFS during your appointment date.
Applicants exempted from paying the Visa Fee:
- Children under 6 years
- School pupils, students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training
- Researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research, as per specific rules
- Applicants related to (spouse, descendant or dependent ascendant of) EEA citizen who are exercising their right to free movement (on production of relevant certificates (birth certificate,
                                                                            marriage certificate, etc.)
- Applications for Entry visas (d-visa) for skilled workers (including their family members) who have received a concrete offer of employment.
- In certain cases, the fee may be waived or reduced in accordance with national legislation, when this would serve to promote cultural, foreign policy or development policy interests,
                                                                            other areas of vital public interest, or for humanitarian reasons.
At VFS, you may avail value-added services which are optional. For details please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/mmr/en/nor/buy-services).

## Documents Required
Checklist for Seafarer Visa to Norway
Power of Attorney Form
Important Note:
All documents must be in A4 paper size
NO staples, pins.
All documents in Burmese language mark (*) on checklist need to be notarized copy at local authority first and be translated to English language.
The documents in Burmese language without mark (*) on checklist are strongly encouraged to be translated into English or Norwegian.

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Standards:
All visa applications must include one recent photograph in color meeting the compulsory internationally agreed standards.
The basic rules are simple:
- The photo must not be older than 6 months
- Taken against a plain light background
- Size 35mm by 45mm (IMPORTANT: Passport photos in U.S. format are not accepted)
- Face must cover 2/3 of photo and looking straight forward
- Neutral expression please and mouth must be closed (no obvious grinning or raised eyebrows)
- Eyes must be on the middle line or in upper half of the photograph
- Hair must not cover face
- No reflection or glare on your glasses and the frames should not cover your eyes (you may find it easier to remove your glasses)
- The photograph must be in sharp focus and clear
- Have a strong definition between the face and background
- Printed professionally
- Eyes open and clearly visible
- Full head, without any head covering unless it is worn for religious beliefs or medical reasons
- CHILDREN: photographs of children must show a clear image that is a true likeness of the child. The eyes should be open and if you need to support a baby’s head, it is important, that your hand cannot be seen in the photograph.

## Processing Time
The handling time of the various applications will depend on the case load. The handling time is calculated from the moment an application has been registered in the visa handling system.
For Schengen visa, visa applications shall be decided on within 15 calendar days after the embassy or consulate has received the application. The decision period may be extended to 45 days when further consideration is needed. 

## Download Forms
You must fill out your visa application form by clicking here (https://www.udi.no/en/).

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The purpose of the au pair scheme is cultural exchange. As au pairs, young people can improve their language skills and knowledge of Norway and Norwegian society by living with a Norwegian
                                                                        family. In return, the au pair performs services such as light housework and child care for the host family. You can be granted a residence permit as an au pair for a maximum period
                                                                        of two years. If the offer of employment is for less than two years, you can only be granted a residence permit for the period covered by the employment offer.
For more information about au pair permits, please click here (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/au-pair/)

Overview
Children whose parents live in Norway are entitled to go to Norway to live with them provided that certain criteria are met. 
Requirements of the parents
- Both parents live in Norway and have legal residence here, or
- One of the parents lives in Norway and has sole parental responsibility, or
- One of the parents lives in Norway and the other parent has agreed to the child moving to Norway (if they have joint parental responsibility)
If the case concerns an adopted child, the Norwegian Directorate of Children, Youth and Family Affairs must have agreed to the adoption before the child enters Norway
For more information about family immigration, please click here (http://www.udi.no/Norwegian-Directorate-of-Immigration/Central-topics/Family-immigration/)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Children whose parents live in Norway are entitled to go to Norway to live with them provided that certain criteria are met. 
Requirements of the parents
- Both parents live in Norway and have legal residence here, or
- One of the parents lives in Norway and has sole parental responsibility, or
- One of the parents lives in Norway and the other parent has agreed to the child moving to Norway (if they have joint parental responsibility)
If the case concerns an adopted child, the Norwegian Directorate of Children, Youth and Family Affairs must have agreed to the adoption before the child enters Norway
For more information about family immigration, please click here (http://www.udi.no/Norwegian-Directorate-of-Immigration/Central-topics/Family-immigration/)

## Visa Fees
For the complete list of visa fees for residence permit, please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/). Note that the visa fees must be
                                                                        paid online using a credit or debit card when you fill in the form.
Aside from the visa fee, VFS will collect a service fee of EUR 75 for each visa application. The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay EUR 67.5 to cover the Resubmission Courier. VFS accepts payment in cash at the Norway counter in VFS during your appointment date.
Applicants exempted from paying the Visa Fee:
- Children under 6 years
- School pupils, students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training
- Researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research, as per specific rules
- Applicants related to (spouse, descendant or dependent ascendant of) EEA citizen who are exercising their right to free movement (on production of relevant certificates (birth certificate,
                                                                            marriage certificate, etc.)
- Applications for Entry visas (d-visa) for skilled workers (including their family members) who have received a concrete offer of employment.
- In certain cases, the fee may be waived or reduced in accordance with national legislation, when this would serve to promote cultural, foreign policy or development policy interests,
                                                                            other areas of vital public interest, or for humanitarian reasons.
At VFS, you may avail value-added services which are optional. For details please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/mmr/en/nor/buy-services).

## Documents Required
- Family immigration for children who have parents in Norway (http://www.udi.no/en/checklists-container/family/vanlig-familie/checklist-for-family-immigration-for-children-who-have-parents-in-noway/?c=mmr)
- Checklist for you who had one parent who was a Norwegian citizen when you were born (http://www.udi.no/en/checklists-container/family/vanlig-familie/checklist-for-you-who-had-one-parent-who-was-a-norwegian-citizen-when-you-were-born/?c=mmr)
- Family immigration for child between 18 and 21 who have previously had a lengthy stay in Norway with a residence permit (http://www.udi.no/en/checklists-container/family/vanlig-familie/checklist-for-family-immigration-for-child-between-18-and-21-who-have-previously-had-a-lengthy-stay-in-norway-with-a-reidence-permit/?c=mmr)
- Family immigration for children over the age of 18 who are financially dependent on their parents (http://www.udi.no/en/checklists-container/family/vanlig-familie/checklist-for-family-immigration-for-children-over-the-age-of-18-who-are-financially-dependent-on-their-parents/?c=mmr)

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Standards:
All visa applications must include one recent photograph in color meeting the compulsory internationally agreed standards.
The basic rules are simple:
- The photo must not be older than 6 months
- Taken against a plain light background
- Size 35mm by 45mm (IMPORTANT: Passport photos in U.S. format are not accepted)
- Face must cover 2/3 of photo and looking straight forward
- Neutral expression please and mouth must be closed (no obvious grinning or raised eyebrows)
- Eyes must be on the middle line or in upper half of the photograph
- Hair must not cover face
- No reflection or glare on your glasses and the frames should not cover your eyes (you may find it easier to remove your glasses)
- The photograph must be in sharp focus and clear
- Have a strong definition between the face and background
- Printed professionally
- Eyes open and clearly visible
- Full head, without any head covering unless it is worn for religious beliefs or medical reasons
- CHILDREN: photographs of children must show a clear image that is a true likeness of the child. The eyes should be open and if you need to support a baby’s head, it is important, that your hand cannot be seen in the photograph.

## Processing Time
For residence permit application, the Embassy will forward your application to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration for considering. Information about case-processing times is intended
                                                                        as a guide. Some cases make take shorter or longer time to process. For details, please check here (http://www.udi.no/Norwegian-Directorate-of-Immigration/Oversiktsider/Case-processing-times/).

## Download Forms
You must fill out your visa application form by clicking here (https://selfservice.udi.no/).
